title: Kane Griggs
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Kane|more=Griggs}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:KaneGriggs-DE3.png]] |name=Kane Griggs |homeworld=[[Kerensik]]''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Dark Empire 3|''Dark Empire'' 3]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners=[[Kane Griggs's wife]] |siblings= |children=[[Kane Griggs's child]] |affiliation=*[[Botor Income Ministry]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Griggs! Recalculate our exit from hyperspace at ''two degrees'' from their point of transmission!''"
"''Yes, ''sir''! The navicomputer says we ''can'' make that adjustment, sir.|General Lando Calrissian orders Kane Griggs to plot a course for Calamari|}} '''Kane Griggs''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the frozen [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kerensik]] in the [[Botor Enclave/Legends|Botor Enclave]]. Griggs eventually traveled off-world to attend an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[University/Legends|university]], where he studied [[Politics/Legends|politics]] and planned on living a comfortable life as an [[Economist/Legends|economist]]. However, he cared little for Imperial ideology or outside conflicts. During his first year at the university, Griggs met an [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] student named [[Marfa Thorbin]] and formed a close relationship with her, eventually proposing [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] to her. Thorbin accepted and returned home to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to tell her parents, but she was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when the planet was [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] by the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]. Soon after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Griggs quit his job at the [[Botor Income Ministry]] and joined the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]] to avenge Thorbin. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Griggs served aboard the liberated [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]'' as a [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]] and participated in the [[Battle of Calamari]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Kane Griggs was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] born and raised on the frozen [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kerensik]] in the [[Botor Enclave/Legends|Botor Enclave]]. [[Winter/Legends|Winter]] was the only season that Griggs ever experienced until he went off-world to study at an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[University/Legends|university]]. Griggs studied [[Politics/Legends|politics]] at the school, and like many of the other political science students, he hoped for a safe and comfortable bureaucratic job. Griggs was apathetic toward the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and only objected to a few of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order's]] policies. He was more interested in becoming an [[Economist/Legends|economist]] than focusing on his service to the Empire. In his first year at the school, Griggs experienced the [[Autumn/Legends|autumn]] season for the first time, which he found pleasantly colorful. One day while walking back from a lecture, counting leaves, he ran into a fellow student—[[Marfa Thorbin]]. As the two picked up each other's books and began chatting, Griggs offered to buy Thorbin a cup of [[Chav (beverage)|chav]]. Though initially embarrassed at his clumsiness, Griggs was surprised to learn that Thorbin was also counting the leaves. Thorbin explained that she grew up on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], where she experienced many different seasons. She told Griggs that she hoped to be an [[Xenobiologist/Legends|aquatic xenologist]] and one day explore [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] worlds. Griggs and Thorbin ended up in two of the same classes together and began a friendship as study partners. They communicated with each other over winter vacation, and once they met again in the [[Spring/Legends|spring]] semester, they reveled in the new season. Over the years, their friendship became love, and eventually Griggs asked Thorbin to [[Marriage/Legends|marry]] him. Thorbin accepted, and the pair set a date for the wedding while Thorbin returned home to tell her parents. Thorbin, however, was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] when the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] Alderaan, and the pair never had their wedding. ===New Republic service=== {{Quote|Coordinates locked in, General Calrissian. We exit hyperspace in forty-five seconds!|Kane Griggs|}} Griggs spent the next few years working for the [[Botor Income Ministry]] until the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] announced their [[Battle of Endor/Legends|victory]] over the Empire at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|formed]] the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Griggs quit his job and joined up as a recruit in the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]], motivated by his love for Thorbin and hatred against the Empire for her loss. Though some doubted his usefulness aboard [[Warship/Legends|warships]], Griggs was content with the life he lived in the New Republic. He eventually fell in love with and married [[Kane Griggs's wife|another woman]] and fathered a [[Kane Griggs's child|child]]. [[File:KaneGriggs.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Griggs (far left), carries out General Calrissian's orders.]] By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], after the [[Operation Shadow Hand|resurgence]] of the reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], Griggs was serving as a [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]] aboard the ''[[Emancipator]]'', an [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] captured by the New Republic. While the ''Emancipator'' was en route to the planet [[Dac/Legends|Calamari]], Griggs was ordered by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the ''Emancipator'', to plot a course two degrees from the source of a recently intercepted Imperial transmission displaying the devastation of the planet. Griggs acknowledged that the ''Emancipator''{{'s}} [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] could make such a course adjustment. He then proceeded to participate in the [[Battle of Calamari]], where he fought against the Empire's [[World Devastator]]s. ==Personality and traits== While at the university, Kane Griggs planned on living a peaceful life and attempted to stay as far away as possible from conflicts such as the Galactic Civil War. He cared little for the Empire's policies and focused primarily on his studies. He found the autumn season incredibly interesting and was fascinated by the different colors the leaves changed to. Griggs's emotions often influenced his actions, such as after Marfa Thorbin's death, when he was motivated to join the New Republic out of his love for her and hatred of the Empire. If asked why he started fighting for the New Republic, Griggs would cite many reasons but leave out Thorbin, whom he considered his biggest reason of all. Griggs was proficient in [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]], [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]], and [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] operation. ==Behind the scenes== Kane Griggs first appeared as a minor background character in the [[Dark Empire 3|third issue]] of the [[1991]]–[[1992]] ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series written by [[Tom Veitch]] and illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]]. His background was greatly expanded upon in the [[1993]] ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' by [[Michael Allen Horne]]. ==Appearances== *[[Dark Empire 3|''Dark Empire'' 3]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Kane}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Griggs, Kane}} [[Category:Financial professionals]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:New Republic Defense Fleet personnel]] [[Category:Pilots]]
